posts.title: Most sellers think kitchen prep starts with a renovation.

head.caption:
Most sellers think kitchen prep starts with a renovation.

It doesn't. It starts with one corner.

The one where the mail piles up. Where the toaster lives. Where the stand mixer hides. Every kitchen has it.

Clear that one corner — completely — and you change how a buyer reads the whole home. The difference between "someone lives here" and "someone cares about this place."

It's also the cheapest thing on the prep list.

If you're thinking about selling in Highland Park, University Park, or Preston Hollow, send me the address. I'll walk through what I'd actually clear first — and what I wouldn't touch.

#DallasRealEstate #DallasRealtor #HighlandParkTX #UniversityParkTX #ParkCitiesRealEstate #PrestonHollow #DallasLuxuryHomes #MelissaMettryRealEstate #SellerPrep

posts.title: The most expensive seller mistake I see in Park Cities homes isn't choosing the

head.caption:
The most expensive seller mistake I see in Park Cities homes isn’t choosing the wrong agent.

It’s the renovation that didn’t need to happen.

A $40K kitchen 60 days before listing rarely returns $40K. Buyers see a clean, updated kitchen, not a story about how much was spent. Appraisals look at comps, not your remodel receipts.

Meanwhile, $4K of strategic prep, staging the spaces that matter most in photos, clearing the kitchen “stuff zone,” consistent lighting, paint touch-ups, and refreshed landscaping, often delivers the same final sales price.

The difference is whether the money shows up in the buyer’s emotional connection to the home or in your closing statement.

If you’re thinking about renovating before listing in Highland Park, University Park, or Preston Hollow, DM me the address before you start. I’ll tell you what’s worth doing and what isn’t.

#DallasRealEstate #DallasRealtor #HighlandParkTX #UniversityParkTX #ParkCitiesRealEstate #PrestonHollow #DallasLuxuryHomes #MelissaMettryRealEstate #SellerPrep

posts.title: Buying and selling at the same time can feel overwhelming — especially in Park C

head.caption:
Buying and selling at the same time can feel overwhelming — especially in Park Cities, where the next home is rarely on the market when you need it. 🏡

I put together a short guide for sellers who are also buyers. How I time the two transactions, what I check before either home lists, and how I keep you out of "bridge loan or scrambling for a rental" territory.

If a move is on your radar, message me your timeline. This is a good place to start.

#DallasRealEstate #DallasRealtor #HighlandParkTX #UniversityParkTX #ParkCitiesRealEstate #PrestonHollow #DallasLuxuryHomes #MelissaMettryRealEstate

posts.title: Most moves to Dallas start with one question: where? 🤍

head.caption:
Most moves to Dallas start with one question: where? 🤍

From the outside, Dallas looks like one city. In practice, it's twenty different neighborhoods, each with its own pace, school system, commute, and lifestyle. The right one for you isn't on a list — it depends on what your week actually looks like.

So before I send anyone listings, I want to know a few things. What time does your morning start, and where do you need to be by 9? Whose schools matter most — your kids', a parent's place, your office? What does Saturday afternoon look like when nothing's on the calendar?

The answers reshape the map. A young professional family moving from Chicago lands in a totally different part of Dallas than a couple downsizing from New York. Same city, completely different streets.

If you're thinking about a move — anywhere from this summer to next year — send me your timeline, what you do, and what your daily life looks like. I'll come back with the three or four streets I'd actually consider for you.

#MovingToDallas #RelocatingToDallas #DallasRealEstate #DallasRealtor #DallasNeighborhoods #DFW #DallasTexas #MelissaMettryRealEstate

posts.title: Before real estate, I spent years in private banking. 🤍

head.caption:
Before real estate, I spent years in private banking. 🤍

It's where I learned that money decisions are rarely about money.

They're about a daughter starting kindergarten. A parent moving closer. A career pivot that no one outside the family knows is coming. The actual question on the table is almost never "what does the spreadsheet say?" It's "what does the next ten years of our life look like, and how do we not get it wrong?"

That's the conversation I always wanted to be in. And real estate — done with care — is the same conversation. A home is where most of that life is going to happen. The pricing, the inspection, the contingencies, the close — that's the technical layer. Underneath is a family making a decision they will live with every morning.

The work I love most happens in homes, in showings, in pre-listing conversations, and in the quiet middle of a negotiation, when families are deciding out loud what they actually want. I built a practice in Highland Park, University Park, and Preston Hollow, where most of my clients are also making the biggest financial decision they'll ever make outside of a business sale.

If you're somewhere in that conversation — a move on the horizon, a sale you've been putting off, a question you don't yet know how to ask — send me a note. First, I want to understand what matters to you.

#DallasRealEstate #DallasRealtor #HighlandParkTX #UniversityParkTX #ParkCitiesRealEstate #PrestonHollow #DallasLuxuryHomes #MelissaMettryRealEstate

posts.title: Most of the work of selling a house happens before there's a price. 🤍

head.caption:
Most of the work of selling a house happens before there's a price. 🤍

When a family is thinking about listing, the first thing I want to know isn't what they paid for it or what their neighbor sold for. It's what's actually driving the move. A kid starting somewhere new. A parent who shouldn't be alone anymore. A career pivot they haven't said out loud yet. The reason behind the move shapes the strategy more than the spreadsheet does.

I want to know who's in the room when they make this decision — the spouse, the financial advisor, the adult kids who don't live in town anymore. The quiet vetoers are usually the ones who slow a deal in week six.

I want to know what the next house looks like, because a move-up runs at a different speed than a move-out-of-state. I want to know what "I made the right call" would feel like to them, ninety days from now. For some families that's a number. For others it's a closing date, a school start, or knowing the neighbor didn't get a discount.

And I want to know the worst version of how this could go. Inspections that surface something real. An appraisal that comes in low. The buyer who walks at the last minute. We name them out loud at the beginning. They have less power over a sale once they're in the room.

If a sale is somewhere on your horizon — a few months or a year out — let's have that conversation early. It costs nothing.

#DallasRealEstate #DallasRealtor #HighlandParkTX #UniversityParkTX #ParkCitiesRealEstate #PrestonHollow #DallasLuxuryHomes #MelissaMettryRealEstate
